rationale: The one substantive reviewer concern in the ground truth (dsocek's suggestion to derive the component/naming list from an existing declarative source like _packed_modules_mapping rather than hand-maintaining it, since similar naming-conflict workarounds exist elsewhere e.g. Qwen image) is echoed in spirit by Candidate X's comment #1 (derive from pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded list, citing other call sites), giving X real recall and strong actionability with concrete file/line suggestions. Candidate Y is methodical and grep-verified but only re-validates that the diff does what it claims — it raises zero of the design concerns actual reviewers surfaced and offers no actionable follow-up, so despite being very safe/precise it scores low on recall and actionability. Neither review contains a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
